highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.149 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a per-category breakdown to the `/usage` command so you can see exactly what's consuming your limits across skills, subagents, plugins, and MCP servers. The `/diff` detail view now supports full keyboard navigation with arrow keys, j/k, PgUp/PgDn, and more. Markdown output also renders properly, making the interface much more readable.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.147: Pinned Sessions & /code-review Command
highlights.summary: Pinned background sessions (created with Ctrl+T in `claude agents`) now stay alive when idle and survive Claude Code updates, giving you persistent workspaces across sessions. The `/simplify` command has been renamed to `/code-review` and now actively reports correctness bugs, making it a more powerful tool for reviewing your code before committing.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest model in the Opus family. As Claude Code users can select which model powers their sessions, upgrading to Opus 4.7 may bring improved reasoning and code quality to your workflows. Check the Anthropic blog for details on capabilities and availability.

highlights.title: Show HN: Spec-Driven Development Workflow for Claude Code
highlights.summary: This workflow proposes breaking complex coding tasks into structured specs before handing them to Claude Code — first generating requirements, then code analysis, then design, and finally implementing subtasks one by one. This decomposition approach helps reduce hallucinations and keeps the agent focused on well-defined chunks of work. It's a practical strategy for tackling large features or refactors with agentic AI tools.

highlights.title: Session Amnesia: The Hidden Cost of Stateless AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: This article explores how stateless AI coding assistants lose context between sessions, forcing developers to repeatedly re-explain their codebase and decisions — a hidden productivity cost. Understanding this limitation helps you design better workflows, such as using CLAUDE.md files or pinned sessions to preserve context. A must-read for developers frustrated by repetitive context-setting.

highlights.title: Claude Code Tutorial: Beginner to Advanced in 20 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial promises to take you from zero to productive with Claude Code in just 20 minutes, covering beginner concepts through more advanced techniques. It's a compact, time-efficient resource if you want a quick but thorough introduction without committing to a full-length course. Great for developers who already know how to code but are new to AI-assisted workflows.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: This video offers a focused 30-minute deep dive into Claude Code, targeting developers who want to move beyond the basics and use the tool more effectively. Expect coverage of features like agents, context management, and real-world coding tasks that go beyond simple code generation. A solid next step after completing a beginner tutorial.

highlights.title: Microsoft Starts Canceling Claude Code Licenses
highlights.summary: Reports indicate Microsoft is discontinuing Claude Code licenses for some users, which may affect developers relying on Microsoft-provided access to Claude Code. If you're using Claude Code through a Microsoft enterprise subscription, it's worth verifying your access status and exploring direct Anthropic subscriptions as an alternative. This is an evolving story — keep an eye on official announcements from both Microsoft and Anthropic.

tips_of_the_day: Use `/usage` after a long session to see exactly which features (skills, subagents, MCP servers) are consuming the most of your limits — then optimize by reducing calls to your heaviest cost drivers.

Add a CLAUDE.md file to the root of your project with key context like architecture decisions, coding conventions, and frequently used commands. This acts as persistent memory and reduces 'session amnesia' every time you start a new Claude Code session.

Before tackling a large feature with Claude Code, spend 5 minutes writing a short spec in plain English — describe the goal, constraints, and expected output. Feeding Claude Code a clear spec rather than a vague prompt dramatically improves the quality and accuracy of the generated code.
